title: CLI receive side: assemble file chunks and write to disk
description:
## What
Handle incoming FileHeader and FileChunk messages in the CLI receive loop. Assembles chunks, verifies SHA-256, writes file to disk.

## Algorithm
1. In the receive state (where ClearMessage is matched after MLS decrypt), add arms for FileHeader and FileChunk.
2. Maintain a HashMap<Uuid, FileReceiveState> in the chat loop state: struct FileReceiveState { name: String, size_bytes: u64, sha256_hex: String, total_chunks: u32, chunks: Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>>, from_pub_id: String }.
3. On FileHeader { transfer_id, name, size_bytes, sha256_hex, total_chunks, mime_type }: validate name has no path components (reject names containing '/' or '\'); validate total_chunks > 0 and size_bytes <= 10MB; insert FileReceiveState; println!('[recv] receiving {} ({} bytes, {} chunks) from {}', name, size_bytes, total_chunks, short_id(from)).
4. On FileChunk { transfer_id, seq, data }: look up state; if not found, ignore (no header received); validate seq < state.total_chunks; store data at state.chunks[seq]; check if all chunks present: state.chunks.iter().all(|c| c.is_some()).
5. When complete: assemble: let assembled: Vec<u8> = state.chunks.into_iter().map(|c| c.unwrap()).flatten().collect(); verify SHA-256: compute hex(SHA-256(assembled)) and compare to state.sha256_hex; if mismatch println!('[recv] ERROR: file hash mismatch - discarding {}', name) and remove state; if ok: write to ./<name> via tokio::fs::write(); println!('[recv] saved {} ({} bytes)', name, assembled.len()); remove state.
6. Path traversal guard: let safe_name = std::path::Path::new(&state.name).file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()).unwrap_or_else(|| 'received_file'.to_string()); write to safe_name, not raw state.name.

## Defensive programming
- Reject file names with path separators before inserting into receive state
- Verify SHA-256 before writing file to disk
- Limit total in-memory buffer: if total assembled chunks exceed 10MB, log warning and discard
- On seq out of bounds: skip silently (do not panic)
- Never log file content (only name, size, from peer)
- chunks Vec initialized to vec![None; total_chunks as usize]

## Tests
Unit tests for the hash-verify and safe_name logic; E2E via W3 bead.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-cli succeeds; FileHeader and FileChunk arms compile and are wired into receive loop
close_reason: Receive side: FileHeader/FileChunk arms in DELIVER+SEALED_DELIVER paths, assembles chunks, SHA-256 verify, safe_name path traversal guard, writes to disk
